title: DeleteMe vs Incogni 2026: Which Data Removal Service Wins?
description: DeleteMe and Incogni both remove your data from data brokers, but they take different approaches. Here is the full head-to-head comparison for 2026.
content:
## Two Good Services, Different Strengths

DeleteMe and Incogni are the two data removal services worth recommending in 2026. Both send removal requests on your behalf to data broker sites that publish your personal information. But they differ significantly in how they work, how much they cost, and who they serve best.

Here is the full comparison.

## Broker Coverage

**DeleteMe** covers 750+ data broker and people-search sites. This includes major players like Spokeo, BeenVerified, Whitepages, Intelius, and hundreds of smaller regional brokers. Broad coverage matters because your data does not live on just one site. Removing it from the top 50 still leaves it on 700 others.

**Incogni** covers 180+ brokers. This is a smaller scope, but it targets the most common ones. For most users, the sites most likely to have your data are covered. The long tail of 570+ additional brokers in DeleteMe's coverage has lower traffic and less real-world impact on most people.

Edge: **DeleteMe**.

## Removal Method

**DeleteMe** uses human reviewers to verify and complete removal requests. Many data brokers require secondary confirmation steps, like clicking an email link or navigating to a specific opt-out form. Automated tools miss these steps, so the request never completes. DeleteMe's human team catches and completes these.

**Incogni** uses an automated process backed by GDPR and CCPA legal requests. In jurisdictions where these laws apply, brokers are legally required to comply. The legal framework gives automated requests real teeth, especially with EU-based brokers.

Edge: **DeleteMe** in the US where broker compliance is inconsistent. **Incogni** in the EU where GDPR enforcement makes the legal approach very effective.

## Pricing

**DeleteMe** costs $10.75/mo billed annually ($129/year for one person). Family add-ons let you cover additional household members.

**Incogni** costs $6.49/mo billed annually ($77.88/year). That is about 40% cheaper than DeleteMe.

Edge: **Incogni**.

## Geographic Coverage

**DeleteMe** is built around the US data broker landscape. Its 750+ broker database reflects which sites operate most heavily in the US. Non-US coverage exists but is not the focus.

**Incogni** covers both US and EU brokers. The Surfshark team built Incogni with European privacy law in mind, and it has better EU broker coverage than DeleteMe.

Edge: **Incogni** for EU users. **DeleteMe** for US users.

## Reporting

**DeleteMe** sends detailed PDF reports every three months. Each report shows which sites had your data, what was removed, and what is still pending. The reporting is transparent and specific.

**Incogni** provides a dashboard where you can see removal status in real time. The dashboard is useful but less comprehensive than DeleteMe's PDF reports.

Edge: **DeleteMe**.

## Automation

**DeleteMe** requires some user input for certain removals. The human oversight means more success, but it is not fully hands-off.

**Incogni** is fully automated after setup. You enter your details once and the service handles everything with no manual intervention required.

Edge: **Incogni** for convenience.

## Full Comparison Table

| | DeleteMe | Incogni |
|---|---|---|
| Brokers covered | 750+ | 180+ |
| Price/year | $129 | $77.88 |
| US coverage | Excellent | Good |
| EU coverage | Limited | Excellent |
| Removal method | Human-verified | Automated (GDPR/CCPA) |
| Reporting | Detailed PDF quarterly | Real-time dashboard |
| Family add-ons | Yes | No |
| Automation | Partial | Full |

## Verdict

**Choose DeleteMe if** you are in the US, want the broadest possible broker coverage, and are willing to pay more for human-verified removals with detailed reports. If doxxing risk or identity theft is a serious concern and you want maximum thoroughness, DeleteMe is the better tool.

**Choose Incogni if** you are in the EU, want a lower price, or prefer a fully automated hands-off service. Incogni's legal framework is especially effective in Europe, and at roughly half the price it delivers good value for most users.

Either way, using one of these services does more for your data privacy than any manual opt-out process you could realistically complete yourself.
